Party_Details.Name: W&P Piling Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: W&P Piling Pte Ltd was incorporated and engaged in mixed construction activities.
The company was ordered to be wound-up by an order of court.
Purchased IMT AF 18 hydraulic equipment for $740,000.
Entered into a scheme of arrangement to pay creditors 0.40¢ for every $1.00.
Sold the first machine to Wee Poh for $380,000.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether directors can be held legally responsible for transferring company assets without proper accounting.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interest of the company and ensure proper accounting for asset transfers.
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff argued that the directors transferred assets without proper valuation or accounting, worsening the company's financial position. The Defendants claimed the transfers were necessary to alleviate cash flow issues.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the directors failed to act in the company's best interest by transferring assets without proper valuation or accounting, leading to financial detriment.

Party_Details.Name: Chew Yin What

Facts.Text: Chew Yin What was appointed as a director of W&P Piling Pte Ltd.
Approved the sale of the first machine to Wee Poh without valuation.
Involved in the decision to transfer machines to Wee Poh without accounting for proceeds.
Responded to liquidator's demand for payment, disputing the amount owed.
Signed a letter asserting that the adjudicated sum was included in the original debt.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for failing to account for asset transfers and proceeds.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must ensure proper accounting and valuation of company assets during transfers.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendant argued that the asset transfers were necessary for cash flow management. The Plaintiff contended that the lack of valuation and accounting contravened legal obligations.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court determined that the Defendant failed to fulfill his duties by not ensuring proper valuation and accounting, resulting in financial harm to the company.

Party_Details.Name: Lee Kok Swee

Facts.Text: Lee Kok Swee was appointed as a director of W&P Piling Pte Ltd.
Participated in the decision to sell the first machine to Wee Poh without valuation.
Involved in the decision to transfer machines to Wee Poh without accounting for proceeds.
Supported the response disputing the amount owed to the liquidator.
Endorsed a letter asserting that the adjudicated sum was included in the original debt.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for failing to account for asset transfers and proceeds.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must ensure proper accounting and valuation of company assets during transfers.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendant argued that the asset transfers were necessary for cash flow management. The Plaintiff contended that the lack of valuation and accounting contravened legal obligations.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court determined that the Defendant failed to fulfill his duties by not ensuring proper valuation and accounting, resulting in financial harm to the company.

Party_Details.Name: Yeung Chun Keung

Facts.Text: Yeung Chun Keung was appointed as a director of W&P Piling Pte Ltd.
Did not participate in the decision to sell the first machine.
Claimed to have no knowledge of the sale of the five machines.
Examined in court on the plaintiff’s affairs and duties as a director.
Filed an to answer questions put by the liquidator.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for failing to prevent asset transfers without proper accounting.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must exercise due diligence to prevent unauthorized asset transfers.
Party_Details.Application: The Defendant claimed limited involvement and lack of knowledge regarding the asset transfers. The Plaintiff argued that the Defendant failed to exercise due diligence in overseeing company affairs.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court acknowledged the Defendant's limited role but found a failure to exercise due diligence in preventing unauthorized asset transfers.
